content: We can frame an Agent Communication Protocol (ACP) for multi-agent interoperability as a shared "working memory layer" for distributed long-horizon active learning, where structured inter-agent message exchanges act as the retention and rehearsal mechanism that preserves task-critical information across extended, multi-agent learning horizons. This framework would let individual agents forget low-priority local information while offloading and retrieving necessary task context via the protocol, avoiding the common working memory collapse that plagues long-horizon learning.
source_topics: Working memory retention mechanisms for long-horizon active learning
